entries.purpose: Primary working document driving all T1/T2/T3 rebuild work — single source of truth for gap status, effort/risk, and implementation steps across every tracked item
entries.description: 77-item gap register (T1 18 resolved, T2 23 resolved + 3 owner-pending, T3 25 resolved + 8 owner-pending); grading summary per dimension vs A+ target; per-item implementation steps, effort, risk, and definition of done

entries.purpose: Detailed P0/P1/P2 gap register from the 2026-05-17 4-agent audit — provides exact file/line evidence and fix instructions; used to prioritise remediation order
entries.description: 29 gaps (5 P0, 14 P1, 10 P2); 15 of 29 resolved as of 2026-05-19; each item lists affected files, step-by-step fix, and resolution status with commit reference

entries.purpose: Frontend-specific gap register gating pre-polish remediation — records implementation gaps found by overlaying architecture docs against frontend code
entries.description: Gaps classified Critical/High/Medium with source authority, current state, evidence, risk, required remediation, and resolution status; scoped to ui/ codebase (now deleted)

entries.purpose: Point-in-time code-vs-docs health scan (2026-05-16) — identifies stale claims in all 25 backend docs before Phase 2 expansion; provides per-doc health score for each file
entries.description: Doc inventory of 25 backend files with stale claim evidence table; code inventory summary (18 modules, 8 domains, 5 adapters, 15-route API); stale claim table per doc with actual code state vs claimed state

entries.purpose: Gap fill summary establishing what was implemented, partial, and missing in the original 8-module backend — historical baseline reference before Phase 2; carries explicit scope notice
entries.description: Implemented (execution lifecycle, governance, measurement, technical-ops, search-intelligence, BI, recommendation scoring, API hardening, test harness); partial (SERP provider, rendered DOM, metric ingestion, BI import); missing (live DB migrations, real auth, persistent rate limiting)

entries.purpose: Capability gap analysis comparing the pre-Phase-2 repo state against the full SEO OS target model — historical baseline; all maturity figures must be recalculated for the 18-module state before use in planning
entries.description: Overall maturity 11% (commercial 18%, white-hat 6%, self-serve 8%); per-capability coverage matrix across 8 dimensions; biggest gap: no execution/governance/measurement system; biggest risk: polished frontend obscures shallow backend at time of writing

entries.purpose: Authoritative full-product expansion build plan for the 18-module target state — planning authority for all new modules and enhancements; supersedes MASTER_BUILD_SPEC for all planning decisions
entries.description: Architecture principles (5-file module contract); 10 new module specs with full analysis.js/insights.js/actions.js logic; 7 existing module enhancements; integration adapter roadmap; schema additions; 6-phase implementation sequence

entries.purpose: Original product build specification establishing core product principles, positioning, and behavior contract — core principles and UI system authority remain valid; superseded by PRODUCT_SEO_OS_BUILD_PLAN for active planning
entries.description: Product type (unified SEO + ASO), market gaps addressed, 7+ module definitions with purpose/inputs/outputs/acceptance criteria, behavior contract (action-first, not dashboard-only), tech stack decisions; updated 2026-05-17 noting 18-module state

entries.purpose: UI architecture governance — defines the required layered build order for the frontend; must be consulted before any UI work to ensure each layer is frozen before the next begins
entries.description: 8 required layers in freeze order: archetypes → behaviour overlay → market overlay → inspiration patterns → pattern language → design language → design system → component layer → SVG icons → iteration passes; per-phase purpose, inputs, outputs, and freeze criteria

entries.purpose: Historical 7-phase rollout plan establishing the original MVP boundary for the 8-module product — retained as a decision record; superseded by PRODUCT_SEO_OS_BUILD_PLAN Part 7 for all active planning
entries.description: MVP defined as Phase 1 (review + content) + Phase 2 (keyword) + Phase 3 (rank tracking); full suite expansion Phases 4–7; scope: web SEO + ASO; superseded by PRODUCT_SEO_OS_BUILD_PLAN which covers all 18 modules

entries.purpose: Step-by-step operator guide for resolving live incidents — read immediately when an alert fires; covers every expected failure mode on the free-tier Render + Supabase stack
entries.description: 7 scenarios: cold-start latency, DB unreachable, SERP rate-limited, Supabase outage, force restart, credential rotation, and DB backup — each with symptom, cause, and numbered resolution steps

entries.purpose: Defines the binding service level objectives for the production backend — establishes measurable uptime, latency, and error-rate targets and their breach consequences
entries.description: 4 SLOs: availability 99.5% (3.6 hr/month budget), p99 /health <500ms, p99 /run/default <20s, error rate <0.5%/24h rolling window, module timeout rate <1%; error budget policy table; 30-day review cadence

entries.purpose: Session-by-session milestone log and project state tracker — the canonical resume anchor; read this first at the start of any new work session to understand current state
entries.description: Project state header (deploy status, backend QC, rebuild plan progress), milestone table per phase, session anchors with resolved items and dates, suggested next work

entries.purpose: Append-only record of all notable changes following Keep a Changelog format — provides a clean history for contributors and downstream release notes
entries.description: Per-release entries with Added/Changed/Fixed/Owner-pending sections; covers all T1/T2/T3 milestones, doc restructures, backend changes, Flutter wiring, and adapter work from 2026-05-19 to 2026-05-22

entries.purpose: Running log of content implementation decisions — tracks what content rules were applied to which screens so future sessions do not undo prior work
entries.description: Per-module coverage notes for all 9 modules; feature mapping components added (ModuleFeatureMap, FeatureEvidenceBoard, FeatureActionCard); implementation rule (INPUT→ANALYSIS→INSIGHT→PRIORITY→ACTION); feature-focus simplification pass notes

entries.purpose: Controlled refinement pass record — documents each structured iteration pass after structural remediation so polish work stays scoped and does not regress structure
entries.description: 5 passes recorded: structural refinement, hierarchy refinement, design-language application, design-system enforcement, density and clarity tuning — each with goal, inputs, changes, validation, and approval status

entries.purpose: Chronological history of all UI implementation sessions — allows future sessions to resume without drift by showing which phases were completed and what each produced
entries.description: Phase-by-phase summary for all 13 phases: archetypes/mapping, behaviour overlay, market overlay, pattern extraction, pattern library, design language, design system, SVG icon layer, component system, archetype assembly, iteration passes, and implementation sequence

entries.purpose: Historical Play Store market research validating the original product direction — retained as a decision record explaining why the product was positioned as a unified mobile-first SEO/ASO platform
entries.description: Google Play ecosystem signal analysis: market reality (fragmented, no dominant mobile-first platform), 4 observed product types (keyword tools, rank trackers, website SEO tools, multi-tool bundles), review-derived pain points (fragmentation, no actionability, weak workflow)

entries.purpose: GitHub-facing project overview and quick-start guide — the first file any contributor or evaluator reads; must stay authoritative and up-to-date at all times
entries.description: Project structure tree, 18-module table, 27-route API surface, Supabase connection details, env var reference (11 core + 8 adapter vars), npm script reference, deployment and local dev instructions

entries.purpose: Contributor rules enforcing code quality and doc hygiene — read before opening a PR; defines the standards all changes must meet
entries.description: Branch naming conventions, commit message style, pre-commit/pre-push hook rules, 5-file backend module contract, doc update table (DOC_CATALOGUE, PRODUCTION_READINESS_GAPS, PROGRESS, CHANGELOG, BACKEND_DATA_AND_PERSISTENCE, OPENAPI)

entries.purpose: Responsible disclosure policy — tells security researchers how to report vulnerabilities privately and sets response time expectations; keeps security reports off public GitHub issues
entries.description: Email contact, 48h acknowledgement / 14-day critical resolution SLA, supported versions (latest main only), in-scope assets (backend API, auth, schema, governance, credentials), out-of-scope (Render, Supabase, third-party providers)

entries.purpose: Master index of all 88 documents — first place to look before searching the repo; keeps the document set navigable as the project grows
entries.description: Two sections (ops docs, repo/code docs) covering 87 .md + 1 .yaml; each entry has file link, status, purpose, and description; purpose audit progress tracked in header and in scripts/catalogue-audit.json

entries.purpose: Top-level backend specification translating the product build spec into backend scope — single source of truth for what the backend must do, which modules it contains, and what flow it must enforce
entries.description: Backend purpose and coverage (web SEO + ASO), stack constraints (Supabase + Postgres), 18-module table with module key/display name/defaultActive/initialState, per-module responsibility summary, mandatory execution flow (INPUT→ANALYSIS→INSIGHT→PRIORITY→ACTION)

entries.purpose: Per-module boundary contract preventing implementation drift — read before modifying any module to ensure module ownership and responsibility separation is not violated
entries.description: Boundary rules (no merging, no shared-ownership drift), then per-module definitions for all 18 modules covering backend purpose, core responsibility, input/analysis/insight-action/persistence/orchestration relationship, and activation state

entries.purpose: Defines the runtime activation model — distinguishes built from active modules and enforces the invariant that local_seo cannot enter default orchestration without an explicit override
entries.description: Activation model (18 built, 17 default-active, 1 opt-in), DEFAULT_ACTIVE_MODULES list, BUILT_BUT_INACTIVE_MODULES (local_seo only), built vs active definitions, assertModuleCatalogIntegrity() contract, activation override mechanism

entries.purpose: Service layer and orchestration architecture reference — defines domain/data/orchestration separation so implementation stays structured as the module count grows
entries.description: Service layer principles (module ownership, mandatory flow, built-but-inactive support), domain/data/orchestration separation definitions, per-module service contract (run → insights/actions/metadata), 18-module execution order, domain service layer overview

entries.purpose: Persistence architecture specification — ensures Postgres is used correctly as the source of truth and module ownership is preserved in all schema decisions
entries.description: Persistence principles, Supabase role boundaries (Auth/Storage/Edge Functions), 18-module persistence ownership table (records table, defaultActive, migration file), 12-migration inventory, schema conventions

entries.purpose: Catalogue of all 14 shared utility modules in backend/src/core/ — prevents duplication by ensuring developers know what shared infrastructure exists before writing new logic
entries.description: 14 utilities each with file path, purpose, key exports, consuming modules, and design decision: runtimeContext, createProviderAdapter, seoScorer, domainAuthorityScorer, targeting, persistence, recommendationScoring, domainContracts, intentClassifier, moduleInputRequirements, prioritization, rateLimiter, metrics, errorReporter

entries.purpose: Integration architecture reference defining clean provider boundaries — read before adding any new external API adapter to ensure provider concerns stay separate from module business logic
entries.description: Integration boundary principles, 18-boundary catalog table (module key, adapter name, isImplemented, adapter file), detail sections for all 5 implemented adapters (GSC, GA4, PageSpeed, backlink-provider, serp-provider) with credentials, endpoints, and collect paths

entries.purpose: Audit record confirming API hardening compliance across all 27 routes — read before adding new routes to ensure the same envelope, validation, actor-identity, and rate-limit pattern is applied
entries.description: Hardening summary (response envelope, safe errors, request validation, actor identity header, rate-limit headers, workspace headers, versioning, OpenAPI spec); per-endpoint audit table for all 27 routes with validation, auditable-mutation, and envelope status

entries.purpose: Maps all 18 modules to 8 bounded contexts and explains why the domain layer exists — the architectural rationale document for the domain structure; read before touching any domain folder
entries.description: Domain layer rationale (bounded-context structure for growth without monolith collapse), 8 bounded context definitions, full 18-module-to-domain assignment table with activation states, compatibility adapter approach

entries.purpose: Machine-readable OpenAPI 3.1 specification for all 27 API routes — the contract for client generation, Swagger UI, and API documentation; served live at GET /v1/openapi.json
entries.description: 27 paths with request/response schemas, Bearer JWT security scheme, common headers (actor, clientId, workspaceId), reusable schemas (Recommendation, Task, AuditLog, Snapshot, Attribution, BusinessProfile), error response shapes, pagination parameters

entries.purpose: Documents and locks the zero runtime npm dependencies decision — prevents future engineers from casually adding packages without understanding the security and cold-start implications
entries.description: Context (Render free tier, 512MB RAM, cold-start penalty), decision, rationale (attack surface, cold-start latency, auditability, stability), alternatives considered (Express/Fastify/Hono all rejected), consequences, review trigger conditions

entries.purpose: Documents and locks the pure node:http server decision — prevents future engineers from adding Express or Fastify without revisiting the trade-off
entries.description: Context (HTTP server choice), decision (node:http with explicit if/else routing), rationale (ADR 001 consistency, full control, testability), routing pattern (createRequestHandler → OPTIONS → auth → dispatch → sendEnvelope), alternatives considered, review trigger

entries.purpose: Documents and locks the dual-repository pattern — prevents engineers from removing the in-memory path or adding unnecessary DB requirements for testing
entries.description: Context (persistence needs, Render spin-down), decision (dual impl per domain, resolved by context.query presence, JS catalog as activation truth), rationale (CI without DB, graceful cold-start), resolveRepository() logic, alternatives considered, review trigger

entries.purpose: Documents the decision to expose all 4 domain services via direct HTTP routes — supersedes the original 'routeless' decision and provides the complete 26-route inventory as the authoritative reference
entries.description: History (original routeless decision, routes added 2026-05-15), current 26-route inventory table, per-domain service route detail (measurement, technical-operations, search-intelligence, business-intelligence); historical routeless rationale preserved

entries.purpose: Records the intentional existence of two separate intent classifiers — prevents future consolidation attempts by documenting why they must stay at different abstraction layers
entries.description: core/intentClassifier.js (4-intent heuristic token-based, consumed by search_intent_classifier module) vs domains/search-intelligence/intentClassifier.js (7-intent rule-based, consumed by domain services for SERP analysis); why they must not be merged

entries.purpose: Quality check audit record proving all 10 Phase 2 expansion modules meet the 6-criterion contract — the verification record for the SEO OS expansion; read to confirm any Phase 2 module was properly built
entries.description: 6 check criteria (5 files, activation state, flow contract, 3-type tests, createPostgresModuleRunRepository pattern, recordsTable migration match); per-module verdict for all 10 expansion modules; all 10 PASS (60/60)

entries.purpose: Test harness coverage map and CI command reference — read when adding new tests or debugging CI failures to understand which suites exist and what each covers
entries.description: CI commands (test:backend, test:backend:ci), covered areas (schema/repository, governance, execution lifecycle, scoring, measurement, technical fixtures, search intent, API validation), P0 flow coverage list, no-live-API policy

entries.purpose: Unified schema/repository alignment audit — the authoritative record of how repository field names were aligned to the Supabase migration schema; read before modifying any repository to avoid reintroducing mismatches
entries.description: Phase 1 (2026-05-06): 5 systemic field-name mismatches fixed across 8 original modules without schema migration; Phase 2 (2026-05-16): all 10 expansion modules confirmed clean; shared persistence.js helper added
